elements.text: (ii) Ongoing training
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must ensure that each firm obtains appropriate training to keep the firm
elements.label: text

elements.text: apprised of updated Freddie Mac requirements. If the Servicer provides its own standard
elements.label: text

elements.text: training and/or other communication materials to a firm, the Servicer must include
elements.label: text

elements.text: information regarding Freddie Mac’s requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Referral of Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters to firm
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Requirements prior to referral
elements.label: text

elements.text: Prior to referring a Freddie Mac Default Legal Matter to a firm, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: confirm that the firm is eligible to receive a referral by ensuring that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The firm meets the Firm Minimum Requirements, as specified in Section 9501.2
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac has provided a “no objection” determination, as specified in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The firm has executed a contract with the Servicer requiring the firm to comply with
elements.label: text

elements.text: all applicable Freddie Mac requirements, as specified in Section 9501.3(b)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The firm has executed a limited retention agreement with Freddie Mac, as specified in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 9501.3(b)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The firm has completed Freddie Mac training and any additional Servicer training, as
elements.label: text

elements.text: specified in Section 9501.4(a); and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ There are no conflicts of interest with respect to the retention of the firm and referral
elements.label: text

elements.text: of Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters to the firm
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Diversification of referrals
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must diversify its referrals of Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters to an
elements.label: text

elements.text: appropriate number of firms in each jurisdiction to protect the interests of Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: and to mitigate the risks related to a high concentration of Freddie Mac files. In selecting
elements.label: text

elements.text: firms for referrals, the Servicer must consider firm capacity and management of staff to
elements.label: text

elements.text: file ratios.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Bankruptcy and foreclosure matters
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must not refer foreclosure matters directly to trustees listed on the deeds of
elements.label: text

elements.text: trust.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Section 9401.3(b) for additional referral requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv)Providing documentation to firm
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must identify a file as a Freddie Mac Default Legal Matter when sending
elements.label: text

elements.text: the file to a firm. When referring a file to a firm, the Servicer must provide all
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation required to initiate a foreclosure. If the firm requests any additional
elements.label: text

elements.text: information and/or documentation at any time, the Servicer must provide such requested
elements.label: text

elements.text: information and/or documents within three Business Days after receipt of the request or
elements.label: text

elements.text: within such earlier time frame, if necessary, to comply with timing requirements under
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable law or court orders and procedures.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For any Mortgage that the Servicer refers for foreclosure that is subsequently repurchased
elements.label: text

elements.text: by the Servicer, whether voluntarily or involuntarily, the Servicer must notify foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: and/or bankruptcy counsel within two Business Days of the completed repurchase.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Chapter 3602 for additional information about repurchases.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) Contingency plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: All Servicers must have a contingency plan in place, either in the form of a standalone
elements.label: text

elements.text: document or incorporated into policies and procedures, to redirect new foreclosure and
elements.label: text

elements.text: bankruptcy referrals.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Prohibitions related to Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must not require the firm to perform any foreclosure or bankruptcy-related services
elements.label: text

elements.text: on any Freddie Mac Default Legal Matter without compensation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Prohibition against charging for, contracting for or making arrangements to receive
elements.label: text

elements.text: benefits for Servicing obligations
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Servicer, whether acting directly or through an affiliate, service provider, vendor or
elements.label: text

elements.text: outsourcing company, must not directly or indirectly:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Charge Freddie Mac or the firm for any foreclosure or bankruptcy-related Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: obligations, including expenses covered by the Servicing Spread; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Contract or make any arrangements with the firm whereby the Servicer (or its
elements.label: text

elements.text: affiliate, service provider, vendor or outsourcing company) receives, directly or
elements.label: text

elements.text: indirectly, any financial or other benefits (including, but not limited to, payments, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: provision of employees or free or discounted services or products) from the firm in
elements.label: text

elements.text: connection with any Freddie Mac Default Legal Matter or Freddie Mac-owned or
elements.label: text

elements.text: guaranteed Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Section 8103.2(b) for additional information on Servicing obligations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Prohibitions with respect to use of specific vendors, services and/or products
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer, and not a service provider, vendor or outsourcing company assisting the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer in Servicing defaulted Mortgages, must select the firm to handle Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Default Legal Matters, and Servicers must not permit service providers, vendors,
elements.label: text

elements.text: outsourcing companies or others to participate in or influence, in any way, the Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: referral process.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Servicer must not, whether acting directly or through an affiliate, service provider,
elements.label: text

elements.text: vendor or outsourcing company:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Require the firm to contract with or use a particular service provider, vendor or
elements.label: text

elements.text: outsourcing company or to use or pay for a particular service or product
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Refuse to refer a file to the firm because the firm chooses not to contract with or use a
elements.label: text

elements.text: particular service provider, vendor or outsourcing company or chooses not to use or
elements.label: text

elements.text: pay for a particular service or product; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Charge the firm for any aspect of the file referral or management process, including,
elements.label: text

elements.text: but not limited to, the use of connectivity or invoice processing systems (e.g.,
elements.label: text

elements.text: licensing or subscription fees, “click” charges or any other payment) in order for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: firm to provide services necessary to handle Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters (e.g.,
elements.label: text

elements.text: to prosecute the foreclosure or bankruptcy case)
elements.label: text

elements.text: However, a Servicer may require the firm to use certain connectivity or invoice
elements.label: text

elements.text: processing systems, provided that the firm is not required to pay for the use of or access
elements.label: text

elements.text: to such systems.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Section 9501.5(a) for information about use of and reimbursement for
elements.label: text

elements.text: connectivity and invoice processing systems.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9501.5: Servicer oversight and reporting (09/10/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicer use of connectivity and invoice processing system
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicer reporting on Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicer monitoring and management of firm
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Escalation of firm issues to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Servicer use of connectivity and invoice processing system
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Servicer, whether acting directly or through any vendor, service provider or outsourcing
elements.label: text

elements.text: company, may employ electronic monitoring, management, reporting or information and
elements.label: text

elements.text: document delivery processes technology, referred to in this section as a “Connectivity
elements.label: text

elements.text: System,” and an invoice processing system as outlined below.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Connectivity System
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Servicer may employ a Connectivity System to assist with fulfilling Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: obligations such as:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Packaging and referring foreclosure and bankruptcy cases to the firm
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Communicating information and delivering documents between the Servicer and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: firm as well as any other third parties requiring access to the Connectivity System;
elements.label: text

elements.text: and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Managing and monitoring foreclosure and bankruptcy cases
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Servicer uses a Connectivity System:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac will reimburse the Servicer for the actual cost of the connectivity fee up
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the maximum expense limit specified in Section 9701.3(a)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer must provide the firm with use of and access to the identical
elements.label: text

elements.text: Connectivity System
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer must permit or continue to permit the firm to integrate its own
elements.label: text

elements.text: technology systems with the Connectivity System at no cost to the firm; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer must not pass on any Connectivity System related charges to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower or the firm
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Invoice processing system
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Servicer may employ an invoice processing system for managing the submission and
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment of invoices.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Servicer, whether acting directly or through a vendor or outsourcing company,
elements.label: text

elements.text: processes firm invoices electronically:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac will reimburse the Servicer for the actual cost of the invoicing fee up to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the maximum expense limits specified in Section 9701.3(a); and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer must not pass on any invoice processing related charges to the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: or the firm
elements.label: text

elements.text: The amounts specified in Section 9701.3(a) for connectivity and invoice processing systems
elements.label: text

elements.text: are the maximum amounts for which a Servicer may seek reimbursement for the life of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: default (i.e., the duration of the foreclosure, including any Freddie Mac Default Legal Matter
elements.label: text

elements.text: such as bankruptcy).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Example: If a Servicer has already referred a Mortgage to foreclosure and it then becomes
elements.label: text

elements.text: necessary to take action with respect to a bankruptcy related to such Mortgage, or if a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer has already referred a file for bankruptcy and foreclosure has commenced following
elements.label: text

elements.text: the bankruptcy referral, the Servicer may be reimbursed only for one connectivity fee.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Likewise in this scenario, if the Servicer is using an invoice processing system, then the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer may only seek reimbursement for one invoicing fee associated with the foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: and for one invoicing fee associated with the bankruptcy during the life of the default.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Servicer reporting on Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must provide reports related to firm performance, management of foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: and bankruptcy processes, oversight of firm compliance and performance and other related
elements.label: text

elements.text: matters as required by Freddie Mac. Servicers must ensure that all firms retained for Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac Default Legal Matters report data required by Freddie Mac directly to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: accurately and in the time frames prescribed. This includes required daily reporting by its
elements.label: text

elements.text: retained law firms, via the Attorney Data Reporting (ADR) System (see Exhibit 88,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Tools), of key metrics such as:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Milestones during the lifecycle of Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Delays affecting prompt and efficient completion of the Freddie Mac Default Legal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Matter
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Successful loss mitigation activities
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Litigation detail during the lifecycle of certain non-routine litigation matters
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Completion of the Freddie Mac Default Legal Matter
elements.label: text

elements.text: Key metrics generally must be reported to Freddie Mac within 24 hours of occurrence unless
elements.label: text

elements.text: otherwise prescribed in related training materials for the web-based attorney reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: system. Servicers may obtain access to the ADR System and monitor their law firms’
elements.label: text

elements.text: reporting progress by completing the ADR Servicer Access Request Form available on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Default-Related Legal Services webpage at
elements.label: text

elements.text: https://sf.freddiemac.com/working-with-us/servicing/default-related-legal-services.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Servicer has further questions regarding ADR System access, they may contact Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac at Foreclosures@FreddieMac.com.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Servicer monitoring and management of firm
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer is responsible for managing and monitoring all aspects of the firm performance,
elements.label: text

elements.text: providing necessary assistance to the firm relating to Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters and
elements.label: text

elements.text: undertaking all activities required to protect Freddie Mac’s interest in the Mortgage. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must also ensure that the firm is in compliance with applicable Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements and that the firm receives all training and documentation relating to applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac requirements, either separately or as part of the Servicer’s standard training.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Compliance processes
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must develop and have in place policies and procedures regarding oversight
elements.label: text

elements.text: and compliance of firms handling Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters. The Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: have policies and procedures reasonably designed to ensure that firms handling Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac Default Legal Matters are in compliance with the limited retention agreement, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable provisions of the Guide and applicable law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer’s ongoing compliance monitoring must address the following minimum
elements.label: text

elements.text: elements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ongoing eligibility under the Firm Minimum Requirements specified in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Compliance with the limited retention agreement, including the fee and cost
elements.label: text

elements.text: guidelines; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Firm performance and processes necessary to ensure Servicer’s compliance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable Guide requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must conduct periodic compliance reviews and training as appropriate. In
elements.label: text

elements.text: determining the frequency of firm compliance reviews, the Servicer must consider the
elements.label: text

elements.text: overall risk posed to Freddie Mac by the firm (legal, reputational and financial), firm file
elements.label: text

elements.text: volume, performance, any changes in staffing ratios or levels, any litigation against the
elements.label: text

elements.text: firm alleging systemic issues, any media coverage regarding the firm and the prior results
elements.label: text

elements.text: of any firm compliance reviews.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Freddie Mac review of compliance process
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac reserves the right to review the Servicer’s compliance process. Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: may require Servicers to conduct additional compliance activities related to firms
elements.label: text

elements.text: handling Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters, such as additional firm compliance
elements.label: text

elements.text: reviews.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must make available to Freddie Mac upon request the materials relating to
elements.label: text

elements.text: its performance and compliance monitoring of firms handling Freddie Mac Default Legal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Matters, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Information regarding the scope and methodology of the Servicer’s compliance
elements.label: text

elements.text: monitoring
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The schedule of firm compliance reviews conducted
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The identity of any vendors used in the firm compliance reviews
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All documentation from the firm compliance reviews; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All findings, reports or remediation plans resulting from the firm compliance reviews
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition, Freddie Mac may require a Servicer to change the scope of its compliance
elements.label: text

elements.text: process used to monitor firms handling Freddie Mac Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Freddie Mac right to audit firm
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac also reserves the right to directly conduct firm audits and firm on-site visits
elements.label: text

elements.text: as Freddie Mac deems necessary. Freddie Mac audits and visits may focus on items such
elements.label: text

elements.text: as:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Fee and cost compliance
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicer compliance with Freddie Mac requirements, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ High-risk issues, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Compliance with applicable laws
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Reputational risk
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Unsatisfactory results of Servicer firm compliance reviews; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Conflicts of interest involving Freddie Mac-owned or guaranteed Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Escalation of firm issues to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Escalation of issues
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must notify Freddie Mac via e-mail (see Directory 1) within two Business
elements.label: text

elements.text: Days of discovery, or sooner if circumstances warrant, if the Servicer becomes aware of
elements.label: text

elements.text: any issues or concerns relating to a firm (including a specific employee or vendor of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: firm) or a Freddie Mac Default Legal Matter, including, but not limited to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any information regarding a firm that may warrant a firm’s suspension, termination
elements.label: text

elements.text: or Servicer request to transfer Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters to another firm
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Information suggesting legal or reputational risk posed by the firm such as bar
elements.label: text

elements.text: complaints, sanctions or litigation alleging systemic issues with the firm, firm
elements.label: text

elements.text: attorney or firm practices
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Security incidents that compromise the security, confidentiality or integrity of
elements.label: text

elements.text: “sensitive customer information” and that the security incident is related to Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac-owned or guaranteed Mortgages (refer to Section 1301.2(f))
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Actual or alleged fraud on the part of the firm
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Federal, State or local governmental inquiries, including congressional inquiries,
elements.label: text

elements.text: regarding a firm, Freddie Mac-owned or guaranteed Mortgages or Freddie Mac or
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer practices affecting Freddie Mac-owned or guaranteed Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Non-routine litigation (as described in Section 9402.1(b))
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Media inquiries relating to Freddie Mac, a firm or Freddie Mac-owned or guaranteed
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Volume or capacity issues with the firm
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Breach of the limited retention agreement between the firm and Freddie Mac or the
elements.label: text

elements.text: contract between the firm and the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Legal matters such as regulatory updates and specific reporting on certain matters
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e.g., transfer tax matters)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any systemic issues with the firm
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Systemic Servicer issues related to file suspensions and foreclosure holds (e.g., failure
elements.label: text

elements.text: to properly implement new statutory changes); and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any material changes in the ownership, partnership or organization of the firm after
elements.label: text

elements.text: executing the limited retention agreement. Such notifications should include instances
elements.label: text

elements.text: where a named partner leaves the firm or a major practice group separates from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: firm.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Procedures relating to issues and concerns
elements.label: text

elements.text: When a Servicer provides Freddie Mac notice of an issue requiring Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: attention, the Servicer must designate in its e-mail one or more points of contact. Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac may request that the Servicer obtain additional information from the firm regarding
elements.label: text

elements.text: the issue that was escalated to Freddie Mac, and the Servicer must promptly provide the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requested information to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Freddie Mac rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac reserves the right to issue direction to Servicers and firms regarding
elements.label: text

elements.text: escalated issues.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Section 9501.6(c) for more information about Freddie Mac’s reservation
elements.label: text

elements.text: of rights.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv)Escalated issue – confidential information
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any issue that is identified and escalated to or by Freddie Mac pursuant to this section
elements.label: text

elements.text: (other than non-routine litigation) is considered to be “confidential information” as
elements.label: text

elements.text: defined in Sections 1201.8 and 8101.4(d). The Servicer must comply with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of such sections with respect to treatment of any escalated issue.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9501.6: File transfers, termination and suspension of
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ File transfers, termination and suspension of firms
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Implementing file transfers and/or the termination and suspension of firms
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reservation of rights and remedies for non-compliance concerning litigation
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) File transfers, termination and suspension of firms
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Servicer-directed suspension of referrals, Freddie Mac Default Legal Matter
elements.label: text

elements.text: transfers and terminations
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Servicer becomes aware of information regarding a firm’s handling of Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Default Legal Matters that might warrant a suspension of referrals of new Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Default Legal Matters, the transfer of Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters to another firm
elements.label: text

elements.text: and/or termination of the firm (such as for legal, reputational or operational risk), the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Notify Freddie Mac within two Business Days via e-mail or sooner if circumstances
elements.label: text

elements.text: warrant, as set forth in Section 9501.5(d); and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Conduct due diligence with respect to the issue
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer intends to suspend referrals of new Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters,
elements.label: text

elements.text: transfer Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters and/or terminate a firm, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: provide Freddie Mac with at least five Business Days’ notice (see Directory 1) prior to
elements.label: text

elements.text: implementing the decision. Additionally, the notification must provide Freddie Mac with
elements.label: text

elements.text: the implementation plan for the course of action chosen by the Servicer, pursuant to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 9501.6(b).
elements.label: text

elements.text: For the transfer of Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters, once a Servicer has determined
elements.label: text

elements.text: the eligible law firm(s) that will receive such file transfers, the following must also be
elements.label: text

elements.text: included in the notification to Freddie Mac:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicer name and the six-digit Seller/Servicer number
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The nine-digit Freddie Mac loan number
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicer loan number
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Date of transfer
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Original law firm name
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ New law firm name
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac Default Legal Matter being transferred (e.g., foreclosure, bankruptcy
elements.label: text

elements.text: proof of claim (POC) or bankruptcy motion for relief (MFR)) to the new law firm
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The State in which the Mortgaged Premises is located
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition, the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Upon request, provide Freddie Mac with the reason for the decision and the due
elements.label: text

elements.text: diligence materials or other information supporting the decision
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Inform the firm of the decision; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Keep Freddie Mac periodically updated with respect to the status of implementation
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the decision
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Section 9501.6(b) for additional information relating to implementation of
elements.label: text

elements.text: terminations, transfer of Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters and suspensions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Freddie Mac-directed suspension of referrals, matter transfers and terminations
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac may direct the Servicer to initiate an investigation of a firm if Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: becomes aware of information that might warrant a suspension of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Referrals of new Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The transfer of Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Termination of the firm
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac also may conduct due diligence and investigations as necessary. Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac may instruct Servicers to suspend some or all referrals of new Freddie Mac Default
elements.label: text

elements.text: Legal Matters, transfer some or all existing Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters or
elements.label: text

elements.text: terminate a firm.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the event of a decision by Freddie Mac to suspend referrals of new Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Default Legal Matters, transfer Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters or terminate a firm,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Inform the Servicer of the decision and provide direction with respect to required
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer actions, including direction with respect to transfers of Freddie Mac Default
elements.label: text

elements.text: Legal Matters
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Inform the firm of the decision and provide direction to the firm with respect to
elements.label: text

elements.text: required firm actions; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Terminate the limited retention agreement between Freddie Mac and the firm, as
elements.label: text

elements.text: appropriate
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Documentation of due diligence review
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must maintain documentation of the due diligence review, the Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: decision and all other information supporting the decision for a period of seven years
elements.label: text

elements.text: after such decision.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Implementing file transfers and/or the termination and suspension of firms
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Implementation plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Prior to implementing any decision to terminate a contract with a firm, suspend referrals
elements.label: text

elements.text: of new Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters and/or transfer Freddie Mac Default Legal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Matters from a firm, the Servicer must develop an implementation plan which addresses:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ File transfers
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The capacity of other eligible firms in the jurisdiction to handle additional Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac Default Legal Matters and/or transferred Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Proration of fees and costs between the transferor and transferee firms
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Contract provisions during any transition period, including insurance; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Other issues as necessary
elements.label: text

elements.text: The implementation plan must take into account any legal, operational or reputational
elements.label: text

elements.text: risks that may arise during the transition period and must address these risks in the most
elements.label: text

elements.text: cost-efficient and effective manner. Freddie Mac reserves the right to require the
elements.label: text

elements.text: modification of the implementation plan and provide additional Servicer requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: relating to the termination of any firm, the suspension of referrals of new Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Default Legal Matters and the transfer of Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Servicer monitoring of implementation plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must take all necessary steps to ensure that the implementation plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: proceeds in an orderly manner and that all Freddie Mac interests are protected during the
elements.label: text

elements.text: implementation. Such steps include, but are not limited to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Transferring files relating to Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters to eligible firms
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Addressing any issues arising from the transfer of files, the suspension of referrals
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the termination of a firm
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reporting periodically to Freddie Mac on the status of the plan, including such details
elements.label: text

elements.text: as how many files are transferred to each new firm, which new firms receive the files
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the timing of transfers; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Such other details as requested by Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers may not charge Freddie Mac or Borrowers for any fees or costs associated with
elements.label: text

elements.text: transferring Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters, and such amounts may not be added to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower Mortgage balances.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Freddie Mac’s rights to manage termination, suspension and/or file transfers
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac may decide, in its sole discretion, that the legal, operational or reputational
elements.label: text

elements.text: risks necessitate Freddie Mac’s management of the:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Termination of any firm with respect to its handling of Freddie Mac Default Legal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Matters
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Suspension of referrals of Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters to a firm; and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Transfers of files relating to Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters
elements.label: text

elements.text: In such case, the Servicer must cooperate with Freddie Mac in such management and
elements.label: text

elements.text: provide all necessary documentation, files and information as requested by Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Reservation of rights and remedies for non-compliance concerning litigation
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Reservation of rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac reserves the right to direct and control all litigation involving a Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: loan. The Servicer and firm handling the litigation must cooperate fully with Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the prosecution, defense or handling of the matter.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition, Freddie Mac reserves the right to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Select the foreclosure counsel for a particular case, whether the case is routine or non￾routine litigation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Direct and manage the actions taken by the foreclosure counsel on a case-by-case or
elements.label: text

elements.text: individual jurisdiction basis
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Assess additional compensatory fees against the Servicer and/or seek repayment of
elements.label: text

elements.text: losses, costs or damages from the Servicer sustained due to errors, omissions or
elements.label: text

elements.text: delays by the Servicer or its agent; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Direct and manage the actions taken by Servicers and firms relating to escalated
elements.label: text

elements.text: issues specified in Section 9501.5(d)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Remedies for non-compliance
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Servicer fails to comply with the provisions under Chapter 9501, Freddie Mac, in its
elements.label: text

elements.text: sole discretion and in addition to any other remedies specified in the Guide or the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer’s other Purchase Documents, reserves the right to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Refuse to reimburse the Servicer for any legal fees and costs
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Offset the entire legal fee from future foreclosure expenses otherwise eligible for
elements.label: text

elements.text: reimbursement from Freddie Mac or seek the Servicer’s reimbursement of the entire
elements.label: text

elements.text: legal fee with interest if Freddie Mac has already reimbursed the Servicer for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: costs involved in the particular foreclosure or bankruptcy
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Require the Servicer to reimburse the firm or Freddie Mac for any prohibited
elements.label: text

elements.text: payments or other financial benefits
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Prohibit the Servicer from contracting, directly or through any service provider,
elements.label: text

elements.text: vendor or outsourcing company, with a firm with respect to products or services
elements.label: text

elements.text: ancillary to a foreclosure or bankruptcy case
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Prohibit the Servicer from contracting with the service provider, vendor or
elements.label: text

elements.text: outsourcing company involved in the prohibited activities with respect to Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac-owned or guaranteed Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Seek Servicer repayment of losses, costs or damages sustained by Freddie Mac due to
elements.label: text

elements.text: errors by the Servicer or its agent and/or require repurchase of impacted Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 9601: Deficiency Recovery
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: 9601.1: Deficiency recovery and management
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Working with vendors to collect deficiencies
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Assigning deficiency rights after the foreclosure sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Charging off the deficiency
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Working with vendors to collect deficiencies
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac may use vendors to assist in the collection of deficiencies. The Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: assist such vendors to obtain any necessary case file documentation upon the vendor’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: request. This would include data reporting or case file documentation that firms, selected and
elements.label: text

elements.text: retained by the Servicer to handle Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters, may have obtained in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the course of handling a particular case. At the time the vendor requests documentation for
elements.label: text

elements.text: this purpose, as part of the request, the vendor will provide a letter of authorization from
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac to obtain such information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer may only execute deficiency assignment documents that transfer deficiency
elements.label: text

elements.text: rights from the Servicer to Freddie Mac when requested by an MI or third-party vendor.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Section 9601.1(b) for requirements on assigning deficiency rights.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Assigning deficiency rights after the foreclosure sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Mortgage has mortgage insurance and is not covered by any other credit enhancement,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer must not execute any assignment of the right to pursue a deficiency or
elements.label: text

elements.text: assignment of a deficiency judgment to an MI or a third party. These documents must be sent
elements.label: text

elements.text: directly to Freddie Mac (see Directory 5). Freddie Mac will coordinate the execution of
elements.label: text

elements.text: these assignment documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer may execute deficiency assignment documents that transfer deficiency rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: from the Servicer to Freddie Mac when requested by an MI or third-party vendor.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For all other issues related to assigning deficiency rights after foreclosure sale, Servicers
elements.label: text

elements.text: should direct their questions to Freddie Mac (see Directory 5).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Charging off the deficiency
elements.label: text

elements.text: The amount that Freddie Mac has determined to be charged off will be reflected on the Draft
elements.label: text

elements.text: Report. The Servicer must review the Draft Report and report any discrepancies between its
elements.label: text

elements.text: records and the amount on the Draft Report to Freddie Mac via the Freddie Mac Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Data Corrections tool (see Exhibit 88, Servicing Tools) within 30 calendar days following
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s posting of the amount to the Draft Report. Servicers may access the Draft
elements.label: text

elements.text: Report through the Freddie Mac Cash Manager tool (see Exhibit 88).
elements.label: text

elements.text: When reporting a discrepancy, Servicers must input the calculation used to determine the
elements.label: text

elements.text: variance and upload any documentation to support the request in the Servicing Data
elements.label: text

elements.text: Corrections tool.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will process, at its discretion, discrepancies that are submitted more than 60
elements.label: text

elements.text: calendar days after the initial adjustment is posted to the Draft Report. Such discrepancies
elements.label: text

elements.text: may be subject to a contract noncompliance and contract change compensatory fee (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 8303.5(i)). If the postsettlement correction request is denied, the Servicer may be
elements.label: text

elements.text: liable for any additional losses.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 9602: Post-Sale Reporting to Other
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Entities
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9602.1: Post-foreclosure reporting and compliance
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicer reporting of foreclosure sales to credit repositories
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicer reporting to the Internal Revenue Service (IRS)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Servicer reporting of foreclosure sales to credit repositories
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must report all foreclosure sales to the credit repositories listed in Exhibit 51,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Credit Repositories and Information to Report, according to the requirements in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Servicer reporting to the IRS
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must report the acquisition or abandonment of the property to the IRS according
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the requirements in Section 8106.2(b) on IRS Form 1099-A, Acquisition or Abandonment
elements.label: text

elements.text: of Secured Property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the event that deficiency rights were not preserved during the foreclosure process as may
elements.label: text

elements.text: be allowed under Section 9301.6(a)(ii), the Servicer must also report the cancelation of debt
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the IRS according to the requirements in Section 8106.2(c) on IRS Form 1099-C,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cancellation of Debt.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 9603: REO
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: 9603.1: General REO Servicing requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ General Servicer requirements for REO properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Required REO documentation and reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Notifying the taxing authority/HOA of REO
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Property insurance for REO
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Canceling utilities for REO
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) General Servicer requirements for REO properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: Once Freddie Mac has acquired a property in REO and the Servicer has successfully reported
elements.label: text

elements.text: the foreclosure sale or deed-in-lieu of foreclosure via Resolve® and the Loan Level Reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: tool (see Exhibit 88, Servicing Tools) pursuant to Section 9603.1(b), the Servicer will no
elements.label: text

elements.text: longer have the responsibility for the following REO activities, including for properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: located in States that have a redemption, confirmation process or ratification of sale:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Securing, maintaining, inspecting, protecting and preserving the property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Making advances to superior lienholders, including condominium/homeowners
elements.label: text

elements.text: association (HOA) or Cooperative Corporation assessments (see Chapter 8801 for special
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing requirements for Cooperative Share Loans), Condominium Unit maintenance
elements.label: text

elements.text: fees or Cooperative Unit Maintenance Fees and ground rents
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Evicting the occupants in the property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Marketing and rehabilitating the REO
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers are responsible for the following activities until the sale of the REO by Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac or the MI, FHA, RHS or VA:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Filing and concluding FHA, RHS and VA claims, if applicable. (Freddie Mac will file MI
elements.label: text

elements.text: claims.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Filing and concluding property insurance claims, if applicable, and applying for premium
elements.label: text

elements.text: refunds, in accordance with Section 9603.1(d). The Servicer is required to cancel any
elements.label: text

elements.text: existing property insurance policies no later than 14 days after the foreclosure sale or
elements.label: text

elements.text: deed-in-lieu of foreclosure has been successfully reported to Freddie Mac. Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: may instruct the Servicer to maintain property insurance (even if the property is vacant
elements.label: text

elements.text: and has no claimable damage). If Freddie Mac makes this request, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: continue the insurance coverage until Freddie Mac notifies the Servicer that Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: has sold the REO.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Referring all inquiries and offers regarding purchase of the REO to Freddie Mac (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Directory 6) within one Business Day of the inquiry or offer
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Fulfilling all requests made by Freddie Mac, including attorney selection. If the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: requires the assistance of an attorney in fulfilling any of the obligations set forth in this
elements.label: text

elements.text: chapter, the Servicer must use an attorney who meets the criteria in Section 9501.2.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Taking such action that Freddie Mac may request regarding a property
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers may review the REO Overview report, accessible via the “REO” tile of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer’s Servicer Performance Profile (see Exhibit 88), for the property status. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer may also call Customer Service at 800-FREDDIE to obtain this information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will not send the Servicer a written notice of the sale of the REO.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Required REO documentation and reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: When reporting an REO, the Servicer must report the foreclosure sale result “REO” in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Resolve no later than one Business Day immediately following the date of the foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale. Refer to Section 9301.9(a).
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must submit to Freddie Mac (see Directory 6) any information Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: requests from the Mortgage file within 15 Business Days of the date of its request. (Refer to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 3302.1(c) for Freddie Mac’s record retention requirements for Mortgage files on
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosed Mortgages.) Additionally, for a Cooperative Unit in REO, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: submit the information listed in Section 8801.4(d) if requested by Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Notifying the taxing authority/HOA of REO
elements.label: text

elements.text: Within five days of successfully reporting the foreclosure sale or deed-in-lieu of foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: via Resolve, the Servicer must notify all organizations that require notice of Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: ownership of the property to ensure that statements are sent to the appropriate location. This
elements.label: text

elements.text: includes, but is not limited to, the taxing authority, condominium association, municipality,
elements.label: text

elements.text: HOA or Cooperative Corporation (see Chapter 8801 for special Servicing requirements for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Share Loans), and any organization as required by law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the Servicer contacts these organizations, the Servicer must update the remitter’s name
elements.label: text

elements.text: as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation
elements.label: text

elements.text: c/o Radian Real Estate Management
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6330 South 3000 East, Suite 600
elements.label: text

elements.text: Salt Lake City, UT 84121
elements.label: text

elements.text: Important: Freddie Mac will pay the property taxes, condominium/HOA or Cooperative
elements.label: text

elements.text: Corporation assessments, Condominium Unit maintenance fees or Cooperative Unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: Maintenance Fees and ground rents, as applicable, as they become due after the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: successfully reports the foreclosure sale or the deed-in-lieu of foreclosure. Freddie Mac may
elements.label: text

elements.text: retain a vendor to conduct such activities.
elements.label: text

elements.text: There are special requirements for REO located in California. California taxing authorities
elements.label: text

elements.text: reassess properties for supplemental taxes from the foreclosure sale date to the sale date of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the REO. Also, taxing authorities often generate a supplemental tax statement long after the
elements.label: text

elements.text: REO sale date. Therefore, the Servicer must notify the taxing authority in writing to send any
elements.label: text

elements.text: supplemental tax bills to Freddie Mac at the address above within five days following the
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure sale or the deed-in-lieu of foreclosure date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Property insurance for REO
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must maintain or cancel the existing property insurance policy according to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements in this section and Section 8202.6. Additionally, the Servicer must assist in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: filing of a property insurance claim, when applicable, according to requirements in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9603.1(d)(ii) below.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Maintaining or canceling property insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must take all actions required in the mortgage clause of all applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: property insurance policies, including, but not limited to, providing all notices to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurer required under such clause in order to preserve the coverage and its maximum
elements.label: text

elements.text: benefits for the Servicer and/or Freddie Mac as mortgagee. The Servicer must cancel
elements.label: text

elements.text: such policies within 14 days after the foreclosure sale or deed-in-lieu of foreclosure has
elements.label: text

elements.text: been reported to Freddie Mac, even if there is claimable damage to the property or if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property has sold.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Filing claims
elements.label: text

elements.text: A property recovery firm will handle the insurance claim filing process on Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: behalf. The Servicer must provide that firm with the necessary information to file and
elements.label: text

elements.text: process the claim within three Business Days of their request for such information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Obtaining a refund for the unearned insurance premiums
elements.label: text

elements.text: After canceling the policy, the Servicer must submit a request for the unearned portion of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the insurance premium to the insurance company for any premium that the Servicer paid
elements.label: text

elements.text: on behalf of the Borrower, if applicable. The Servicer must offset any refund it receives
elements.label: text

elements.text: from the insurance company against expenses the Servicer requests via PAID (Payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: Automated Intelligent and Dynamic) (see Exhibit 88).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Canceling utilities for REO
elements.label: text

elements.text: Once the Servicer has successfully reported the foreclosure sale or deed-in-lieu of foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: to Freddie Mac, Freddie Mac or its agent/vendor will transfer the utilities to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer can obtain reimbursement for any utility bills it paid that Freddie Mac approved
elements.label: text

elements.text: in advance or the Servicer incurred up to the foreclosure sale or deed-in-lieu of foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: pursuant to the process for reimbursement of expenses in Section 9603.3(d).
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9603.2: Filing FHA/VA/RHS and primary mortgage
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: insurance claims (09/10/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ FHA/VA/RHS claim filing
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Primary mortgage insurance claim filing
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Offers to purchase the property with a pending mortgage insurance claim
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Receipt of claim payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reduced, suspended or denied claims
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Remittance of primary mortgage insurance claim payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) FHA/VA/RHS claim filing
elements.label: text

elements.text: On a Mortgage that is insured by the FHA or guaranteed by the VA or RHS, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must process the claim for FHA insurance or VA or RHS guaranty benefits within the
elements.label: text

elements.text: agency’s established time frames and according to the agency’s requirements to ensure that
elements.label: text

elements.text: the claim recipient receives the maximum claim payment amount.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) FHA, VA or RHS claim filing for Mortgages subject to a recourse obligation,
elements.label: text

elements.text: including indemnification
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must file the claim to ensure that payment is made directly to the Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Because the Servicer is the claim recipient, the Servicer should ensure that it receives the
elements.label: text

elements.text: maximum claim payment amount to apply towards any expenses incurred, as Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac will not reimburse the Servicer for expenses incurred on Mortgages subject to a
elements.label: text

elements.text: recourse obligation. (See Section 9701.1(d) on Freddie Mac’s rights.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) FHA, VA or RHS claim filing for Mortgages not subject to a recourse obligation or
elements.label: text

elements.text: indemnification
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must file the claim in Freddie Mac’s name to ensure that payment is made
elements.label: text

elements.text: directly to Freddie Mac (see Directory 6). Because Freddie Mac is the claim recipient,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer should ensure that Freddie Mac receives the claim payment, in the maximum
elements.label: text

elements.text: claim payment amount, before seeking reimbursement for expenses incurred on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Primary mortgage insurance claim filing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will file a claim for loss with the MI if the Mortgage is covered by primary
elements.label: text

elements.text: mortgage insurance and will manage the claims payment process with the MI. The Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must provide all information and documentation pertaining to the claim to the MI no later
elements.label: text

elements.text: than 60 days after the foreclosure sale, short sale or acceptance of a deed-in-lieu of
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure or within any shorter time frame as specified by the mortgage insurance master
elements.label: text

elements.text: policy or by Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Offers to purchase the property with a pending mortgage insurance claim
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Servicer that receives an inquiry or an offer regarding purchase of an REO property must
elements.label: text

elements.text: contact its Freddie Mac REO claim specialist (see Directory 6) within one Business Day of
elements.label: text

elements.text: receiving the inquiry or offer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Receipt of claim payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer receives a claim payment intended for Freddie Mac, whether from the MI or
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable entity (see Section 9603.2(a) on FHA/VA/RHS claim filing and Section 9603.2(b)
elements.label: text

elements.text: on primary mortgage insurance claim filing), the Servicer must send the funds to Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac (see Directory 6) within 10 Business Days after the Servicer receives them. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must also include all supporting documentation relating to the claim payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: received from the MI or applicable entity, including a copy of the claim payment check.
elements.label: text

elements.text: However, if the Mortgage insured by the FHA or guaranteed by the VA or RHS is subject to
elements.label: text

elements.text: a recourse obligation, including indemnification, the Servicer does not need to send the funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Reduced, suspended or denied claims
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the claim payment amount is reduced, suspended or denied by the MI due to the Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: actions or inactions including, but not limited to, failure to comply with the Guide or
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable mortgage insurance requirements, then Freddie Mac may exercise its remedies
elements.label: text

elements.text: provided by the Guide and the other Purchase Documents for the amount that is reduced,
elements.label: text

elements.text: suspended or denied. Refer to Chapter 3602 regarding repurchases, repurchase alternatives
elements.label: text

elements.text: and other remedies.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Remittance of primary mortgage insurance claim payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: When Freddie Mac receives the claim payment from the MI, Freddie Mac will remit any
elements.label: text

elements.text: portion of the proceeds due to the Servicer, less any outstanding amounts due to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: from the Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9603.3: Financial and legal obligations (09/10/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Property Condition Certificate (PCC) process
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Condominium/homeowners association (HOA) or Cooperative Corporation assessments,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ground rents and property taxes for REO
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eviction requirements for REO
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reimbursement of expenses incurred on an REO property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Final settlement between the Servicer and Freddie Mac for REO
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Late execution of REO repurchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) PCC process
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer may review the REO Overview report, accessible via the “REO” tile of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer’s Servicer Performance Profile (see Exhibit 88, Servicing Tools), for the property
elements.label: text

elements.text: status, including the PCC completion date. The Servicer may also call Customer Service at
elements.label: text

elements.text: 800-FREDDIE to obtain this information. Freddie Mac will not send the Servicer a written
elements.label: text

elements.text: notice of the sale of the REO.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Condominium/HOA or Cooperative Corporation assessments, ground rents and
elements.label: text

elements.text: property taxes for REO
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) REO located in a Condominium Project, Planned Unit Development (PUD) or
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Project or subject to a leasehold estate
elements.label: text

elements.text: Pursuant to Section 9603.1(a), the Servicer is no longer responsible for paying
elements.label: text

elements.text: condominium/ HOA or Cooperative Corporation assessments (see Chapter 8801 for
elements.label: text

elements.text: special Servicing requirements for Cooperative Share Loans), Condominium Unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: maintenance fees or Cooperative Unit Maintenance Fees, and ground rents, as applicable,
elements.label: text

elements.text: upon the successful reporting of the foreclosure sale or deed-in-lieu of foreclosure to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac via Resolve®.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In special circumstances, Freddie Mac may require the Servicer to pay assessments after
elements.label: text

elements.text: the foreclosure sale date, in order to protect Freddie Mac’s interests.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Reimbursement of duplicate assessments and property tax payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the event the Servicer has complied with the requirements of this section and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: duplication of assessments and property tax payments exists, Freddie Mac will reimburse
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer for its portion of the duplicate payment(s) in accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirement in this chapter and Chapter 9701.
elements.label: text

elements.text: To receive reimbursement for the duplicate payment(s), the Servicer must submit a
elements.label: text

elements.text: request for reimbursement to Freddie Mac via e-mail (see Directory 6) and include in
elements.label: text

elements.text: that request proof of the Servicer’s compliance with the applicable requirements and
elements.label: text

elements.text: proof of the payment of the expenses.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will not reimburse the Servicer for any such duplicate payments if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer does not meet the requirements in this section and/or the requirements in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 9701. In such instances, the Servicer may seek a refund from the condominium
elements.label: text

elements.text: association, HOA or Cooperative Corporation or taxing authority to which duplicate
elements.label: text

elements.text: payments were made.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Eviction requirements for REO
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will conduct the eviction proceedings if the property is occupied. If Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac requests information or documentation from the Servicer pertaining to the eviction, then
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer must provide the information or documentation to Freddie Mac within the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requested time frames.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Reimbursement of expenses incurred on an REO property
elements.label: text

elements.text: For an REO not acquired by a third party at the foreclosure sale or for legal expenses not
elements.label: text

elements.text: legally collectible from the third-party purchaser, Freddie Mac will reimburse the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: for reasonable expenses incurred in accordance with the provisions in Chapter 9701; Exhibit
elements.label: text

elements.text: 57A, Approved Attorney, Foreclosure, Mediation, Postponement Fees and Title Expenses;
elements.label: text

elements.text: and/or those approved by Freddie Mac during the foreclosure process.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Final settlement between the Servicer and Freddie Mac for REO
elements.label: text

elements.text: After Freddie Mac has received all proceeds from the sale of the REO and the Servicer has
elements.label: text

elements.text: submitted all expense reimbursement requests to Freddie Mac through PAID (Payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: Automated Intelligent and Dynamic) (see Exhibit 88), Freddie Mac will calculate and
elements.label: text

elements.text: complete the final settlement of foreclosure expenses between the Servicer and Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will bill the Servicer for the Servicer’s proportionate share of any expenses
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac has advanced during the REO holding period if Freddie Mac is not able to
elements.label: text

elements.text: recover them from the Servicer’s share of the proceeds. Otherwise, Freddie Mac will net
elements.label: text

elements.text: these expenses against the Servicer’s proportionate share of the sale proceeds, and Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac will send the balance of the Servicer’s share to the Servicer. Freddie Mac will also
elements.label: text

elements.text: collect any fees, charges or other amounts the Servicer owes Freddie Mac with this
elements.label: text

elements.text: settlement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Late execution of REO repurchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac may request a Servicer to repurchase a Mortgage post-foreclosure and after
elements.label: text

elements.text: investor reporting ceases and the loan has been transferred to REO. If the Servicer does not
elements.label: text

elements.text: remit proceeds to Freddie Mac in accordance with the time frame and requirements in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 8303.3(f), the Servicer will be assessed a late execution of REO repurchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: compensatory fee.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The compensatory fee will be calculated by multiplying the amount of the remittance
elements.label: text

elements.text: shortage by the highest quoted prime rate on the last Business Day of the month in which the
elements.label: text

elements.text: REO repurchase occurred in the print edition of The Wall Street Journal in its regular column
elements.label: text

elements.text: entitled “Money Rates” plus 3%. If the prime rate is not published, then Freddie Mac will
elements.label: text

elements.text: determine the comparable rate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The amount of the late remittance and the number of days that the remittance is late is based
elements.label: text

elements.text: on a 365-day year and determines the amount of the late execution of REO repurchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: compensatory fee due to Freddie Mac. There is a minimum charge of $50 on any late
elements.label: text

elements.text: remittance. For purposes of calculating the charge, Freddie Mac uses the rate described
elements.label: text

elements.text: above for any late remittances occurring between the first day after the Accounting Cycle and
elements.label: text

elements.text: up to and including the last day of the following Accounting Cycle.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Section 8303.5(h) for the compensatory fee assessed for late execution of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: repurchase request if not an REO.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 9701: Reimbursement of Expenses
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: 9701.1: General expense reimbursement policies
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reimbursement of expenses
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicer expense approval authority
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Request for pre-approval (RPA)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac’s rights concerning expense reimbursement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Expense reimbursement submission time frames
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Reimbursement of expenses
elements.label: text

elements.text: This chapter contains Freddie Mac’s guidelines and requirements for using PAID (Payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: Automated Intelligent and Dynamic) (see Exhibit 88, Servicing Tools) to request and obtain
elements.label: text

elements.text: reimbursement for eligible expenses incurred in connection with a workout or foreclosure of
elements.label: text

elements.text: a Mortgage serviced for Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The expense reimbursement process is governed by the requirements in this chapter, Sections
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2401.1 and 2404.2, Chapter 2405, other Purchase Documents and the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The terms and conditions of the Mortgage documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Applicable law
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Requirements of the FHA, RHS, VA or MI, if applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: If foreclosure proceedings are discontinued, all fees and costs the Servicer incurs must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: collected from the Borrower (unless prohibited by applicable law). The Servicer must not
elements.label: text

elements.text: charge the Borrower for:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any costs or fees that the Servicer has not incurred, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reinstatement or relief fees that are not allowed by applicable law or the Purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: If applicable law prohibits reimbursement from the Borrower, Freddie Mac will share the
elements.label: text

elements.text: expenses with the Servicer in proportion to Freddie Mac’s interest in the Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Servicer expense approval authority
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers may incur legal and other expenses in accordance with the established limits in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exhibit 57, 1- to 4-Unit Property Approved Expense Amounts; Exhibit 57A, Approved
elements.label: text

elements.text: Attorney, Foreclosure, Mediation, Postponement Fees and Title Expenses, and Exhibit 57B,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Approved Bankruptcy Attorney Fees, without Freddie Mac’s prior approval.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) RPA
elements.label: text

elements.text: Prior to the Servicer incurring the expense or expenses, if possible, the Servicer must obtain
elements.label: text

elements.text: written pre-approval from Freddie Mac by submitting an RPA via the RPA functionality in
elements.label: text

elements.text: PAID to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Exceed the expense limits in Exhibits 57, 57A and Exhibit 57B
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Incur certain expenses as set forth in Exhibit 74, Expense and Income Codes for Expense
elements.label: text

elements.text: Reimbursement Requests
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Incur expenses not set forth in Exhibit 74
elements.label: text

elements.text: All RPAs will require the Servicer to submit supporting documentation. Freddie Mac will not
elements.label: text

elements.text: provide pre-approval for, or reimbursement of expenses considered non-reimbursable (as set
elements.label: text

elements.text: forth in Section 9701.4(a)).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Freddie Mac’s rights concerning expense reimbursement
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac reserves the following rights:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Freddie Mac may deny the Servicer’s request for reimbursement or curtail a portion of
elements.label: text

elements.text: such expenses if Freddie Mac does not receive the Servicer’s request within the required
elements.label: text

elements.text: time frames specified in Section 9701.1(e)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Freddie Mac will not reimburse the Servicer for expenses incurred on Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: repurchased by the Servicer or Mortgages subject to full recourse or indemnification.
elements.label: text

elements.text: However, when applicable, the Servicer must submit expenses on any Mortgage that is
elements.label: text

elements.text: pending repurchase. If the Servicer is required to repurchase Freddie Mac’s interest in a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage in accordance with Chapter 3602, Freddie Mac’s calculation of the repurchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: price will include all amounts the Servicer was reimbursed.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. If Freddie Mac does not exercise its right to set off amounts owed to Freddie Mac against
elements.label: text

elements.text: other funds owed to a Servicer, Freddie Mac will bill the Servicer for expenses and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: losses incurred or discovered after the repurchase notification date and/or the date of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s receipt of the repurchase proceeds from the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. If Freddie Mac audits the Servicer’s reimbursement requests and determines that a
elements.label: text

elements.text: previously reimbursed expense was not eligible for reimbursement, Freddie Mac may bill
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer for any amounts due to Freddie Mac on the Servicer’s monthly Servicer
