elements.label: text

elements.text: Non-Performing Loans Invoice.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Section 9102.1 for information on the payment of Servicing Non￾Performing Loans Invoices via an Automated Clearing House draft.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. The expenses submitted on the final claim for a Mortgage insured by the FHA or
elements.label: text

elements.text: guaranteed by the VA or RHS not subject to recourse or indemnification will not be
elements.label: text

elements.text: reimbursed until Freddie Mac has received the full amount of claim proceeds from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable entity.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Freddie Mac will reimburse the Servicer for additional expenses that appear on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: claim forms the Servicer submits to the applicable entity only if Freddie Mac receives
elements.label: text

elements.text: these funds in the claim proceeds from the applicable entity.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. Freddie Mac reserves the right to require the Servicer to obtain competitive bids from
elements.label: text

elements.text: multiple vendors on property preservation work that exceeds the expense limits specified
elements.label: text

elements.text: in Exhibit 57
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Expense reimbursement submission time frames
elements.label: text

elements.text: Expenses for which the Servicer is seeking reimbursement must be submitted in PAID.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers may enter expenses in PAID as they are accrued; however, Servicers will not
elements.label: text

elements.text: receive reimbursement until the mortgage modification, short sale or charge-off has settled
elements.label: text

elements.text: on Freddie Mac systems or the deed-in-lieu of foreclosure, third-party sale or foreclosure sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: has been successfully reported to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Expense reimbursement submission time frames provided for in this Section 9701.1(e) are
elements.label: text

elements.text: not applicable for Mortgages insured by the FHA or guaranteed by the VA or RHS. For
elements.label: text

elements.text: expenses incurred on Mortgages insured by the FHA or guaranteed by the VA or RHS not
elements.label: text

elements.text: subject to recourse or indemnification, the Servicer must submit expenses in PAID after
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac has received the claim payment from the applicable entity (see Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9603.2(a) on FHA/VA/RHS claim filing and Section 9603.2(d) on receipt of claim payment),
elements.label: text

elements.text: but no later than 60 days after Freddie Mac has received the claim payment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Reimbursement of loan modification expenses
elements.label: text

elements.text: With the exception of recordation fees, Servicers must enter all loan modification-related
elements.label: text

elements.text: expenses in PAID that were not capitalized in the modification or otherwise paid by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower (including unrecoverable expenses described in Section 9701.1(e)(iv)) no later
elements.label: text

elements.text: than 90 days after the settlement date in Freddie Mac systems. Recordation fees (expense
elements.label: text

elements.text: code 300003) must be submitted no later than 180 days after the settlement date in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac systems.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Reimbursement of expenses for short-sales, charge-offs, deeds-in-lieu of foreclosure,
elements.label: text

elements.text: third-party sales and foreclosure sales
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must enter the initial expenses related to a short-sale, charge-off, deed-in-lieu of
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure, third-party foreclosure sale or a foreclosure sale in PAID that were not paid
elements.label: text

elements.text: by the Borrower (including unrecoverable expenses described in Section 9701.1(e)(iv))
elements.label: text

elements.text: no later than 45 days after the workout is settled on Freddie Mac systems or the
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure sale is successfully reported to Freddie Mac. Additional expenses must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: entered no later than 60 days after the workout is settled on Freddie Mac systems or the
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure sale is successfully reported to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: During the REO holding period (i.e., the time period from Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: acquisition of the property to the sale of the REO), the Servicer may review the status of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the REO by monitoring the REO Overview Report, accessible via the “REO” tile of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer’s Servicer Performance Profile (see Exhibit 88).
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer may also call Customer Service at 800-FREDDIE to obtain this
elements.label: text

elements.text: information. Freddie Mac will not send the Servicer a written notice of the sale of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: REO. Usually, the Servicer only needs to submit one claim, referred to as the “final”
elements.label: text

elements.text: claim. Any additional claim submissions are referred to as “supplemental.”
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Reimbursement of other expenses
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must enter the initial expenses for the following expenses in PAID no later than
elements.label: text

elements.text: 45 days after completion of the event, and any additional expenses must be entered no
elements.label: text

elements.text: later than 60 days:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Expenses (e.g., legal fees and/or legal costs) considered unrecoverable from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower under applicable federal, State or local law upon reinstatement or payoff of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Mortgage (including unrecoverable expenses described in Section 9701.1(e)(iv))
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Bankruptcy completion on a current Mortgage for which the Servicer obtained
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s written pre-approval to incur the legal expense via the RPA
elements.label: text

elements.text: functionality in PAID pursuant to Section 9401.2(c)(i)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv)Unrecoverable expenses
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will reimburse certain expenses (e.g., legal fees and/or legal costs), as set
elements.label: text

elements.text: forth in Exhibit 74 considered unrecoverable from the Borrower under applicable federal,
elements.label: text

elements.text: State or local law upon reinstatement or pay off of the Mortgage or upon completion of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: mortgage modification.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For any other expense a Servicer believes to be unrecoverable from the Borrower under
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable federal, State or local law, the Servicer must obtain written pre-approval from
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac by submitting an RPA via the RPA functionality in PAID.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9701.2: Property-related reimbursements (09/10/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reimbursement of property taxes
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reimbursement of insurance premiums
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reimbursement of liens
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reimbursement for property inspection and property preservation expenses
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reimbursement of condominium/homeowners association (HOA) or Cooperative
elements.label: text

elements.text: Corporation assessments and ground rents
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Reimbursement of property taxes
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must administer all funds in the Borrower’s Escrow account to pay expenses in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the terms set forth in the Note and the Security Instrument, in addition to
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable federal, State and local laws. The Servicer must maintain sufficient Escrows
elements.label: text

elements.text: and/or verify timely payments of property taxes in accordance with Sections 8201.1 and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will reimburse the Servicer that is in compliance with the requirements of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sections 8201.1 and 9301.6(e) for property taxes that were incurred and paid to a taxing
elements.label: text

elements.text: authority as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For foreclosure sales and deeds-in-lieu of foreclosure, property taxes are reimbursable if
elements.label: text

elements.text: incurred up to 12 months prior to the DDLPI through the foreclosure sale or the deed-in￾lieu of foreclosure date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For short-sales, charge-offs or third-party sales, property taxes are reimbursable if
elements.label: text

elements.text: incurred and paid to a taxing authority up to 12 months prior to the DDLPI through the
elements.label: text

elements.text: payoff date. (Refer to Section 9701.1(e) for a description of expense reimbursement
elements.label: text

elements.text: requests and submission time frames.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer advanced property taxes as required in Section 8201.1 and entered into a
elements.label: text

elements.text: repayment plan for the property taxes with the Borrower, and the Borrower then breached the
elements.label: text

elements.text: repayment plan during the repayment period, Freddie Mac will reimburse the Servicer for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property taxes the Borrower did not pay if the Servicer provides Freddie Mac with
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The repayment plan and the sequence of events
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Evidence that the Servicer initiated foreclosure as required in Section 8201.1
elements.label: text

elements.text: Except as otherwise provided herein, Freddie Mac will reimburse the Servicer for the first tax
elements.label: text

elements.text: penalty incurred on a non-escrowed Mortgage that goes to foreclosure on the condition that
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer has complied with the requirements of Section 8201.1. Freddie Mac will not
elements.label: text

elements.text: reimburse the Servicer for the interest or other charges accrued on delinquent property taxes.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will reimburse Servicers for the first and second tax penalty incurred on a non￾escrowed Mortgage in the State of California only in instances where the Servicer is not
elements.label: text

elements.text: notified of unpaid property taxes on a non-escrowed Mortgage until the second tax penalty
elements.label: text

elements.text: was assessed.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will not reimburse the Servicer for any late fees, interest and penalties other
elements.label: text

elements.text: than:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The first tax penalty on a non-escrowed Mortgage (including interest if the taxing
elements.label: text

elements.text: authority considers interest as a penalty or includes it as part of the penalty)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The first and second tax penalties on a non-escrowed Mortgage in California
elements.label: text

elements.text: In California, properties are reassessed for supplemental taxes from the foreclosure sale date
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the REO settlement date. A supplemental tax statement is often generated long after the
elements.label: text

elements.text: REO settlement date. Therefore, within five days following the foreclosure sale or the deed￾in-lieu of foreclosure date, the Servicer must notify the taxing authority, in writing, to send
elements.label: text

elements.text: any supplemental tax bills to Freddie Mac (see Directory 6). The Servicer must also send
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac a copy of the letter sent to the taxing authority.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Reimbursement of insurance premiums
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will reimburse the Servicer for property insurance premiums if they were
elements.label: text

elements.text: incurred and paid to the insurer up to 12 months prior to the DDLPI.
elements.label: text

elements.text: After the DDLPI, the following insurance expenses are reimbursable in every jurisdiction:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgage insurance premiums are reimbursable if incurred after the DDLPI and before:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The REO acquisition date. The acquisition date is the foreclosure sale date, the date
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the expiration of the redemption period and/or the confirmation/ratification date,
elements.label: text

elements.text: whichever occurs last.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A deed-in-lieu of foreclosure has been successfully reported to Freddie Mac through
elements.label: text

elements.text: Resolve®
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A short sale has been settled on Freddie Mac systems
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Property insurance premiums paid through an Escrow account are reimbursable for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: period up to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Twelve months after the foreclosure sale or cancelation of the policy (whichever
elements.label: text

elements.text: occurs earlier)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ 14 days after the foreclosure sale or deed-in-lieu of foreclosure has been successfully
elements.label: text

elements.text: reported to Freddie Mac through Resolve if the property did not revert to REO
elements.label: text

elements.text: through foreclosure sale; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ For short-sales, charge-offs or third-party sales, 14 days after completion of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: workout settlement
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Section 9701.1(e) for a description of expense reimbursement requests
elements.label: text

elements.text: and submission time frames.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ◼ Lender-Placed Insurance (LPI) premiums are reimbursable for a period up to 14 days
elements.label: text

elements.text: after the final non-REO activity or up to 14 days after the foreclosure sale and or the
elements.label: text

elements.text: deed-in-lieu of foreclosure has been successfully reported through Resolve. Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: will not reimburse the Servicer for LPI premiums for periods in which the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: obtained coverage meeting the requirements outlined in Chapter 8202 was already in
elements.label: text

elements.text: place. LPI premiums for which the Servicer or an affiliated entity received compensation
elements.label: text

elements.text: in violation of Section 8202.6 are not reimbursable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Reimbursement of liens
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will reimburse the Servicer in most instances where the Servicer must pay
elements.label: text

elements.text: expenses that, if delinquent, are or may become First Liens on the property or that if not paid
elements.label: text

elements.text: would result in the subordination of Freddie Mac’s interests, as provided in the Guide. (Refer
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower under applicable federal, State or local law upon reinstatement or payoff of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage or upon completion of a mortgage modification.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Section 9701.1(e)(iv) regarding unrecoverable expenses.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the event of a foreclosure of the property or a bankruptcy of the Borrower, attorneys’
elements.label: text

elements.text: invoices paid by the Servicer pursuant to Section 8103.2(b) must match or exceed the claim
elements.label: text

elements.text: for reimbursement in PAID. Servicers must pay attorneys’ invoices timely and should not be
elements.label: text

elements.text: delaying the payment of attorneys’ invoices until after the expenses have been reimbursed by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will not reimburse the Servicer for attorney’s fees and litigation costs on
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages sold to Freddie Mac with full recourse or subject to indemnification. For
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages sold to Freddie Mac with recourse or subject to indemnification, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: inform counsel retained for foreclosure, eviction or bankruptcy that the Servicer is
elements.label: text

elements.text: responsible for paying all associated fees and costs.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Reimbursement requirements when foreclosure must be restarted
elements.label: text

elements.text: In certain States, if the Servicer must restart a foreclosure proceeding, in whole or in part,
elements.label: text

elements.text: due to an allowable delay as defined in Section 9301.11(b), Freddie Mac will reimburse the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. All of the reimbursable actual costs (per the requirements of the Guide) of each
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure attempt and the completed foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Up to 70% of the approved foreclosure attorney fees for the first foreclosure attempt
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Up to 100% of the approved foreclosure attorney fees for the completed foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additionally, for any foreclosure attempt subsequent to the first foreclosure attempt, except
elements.label: text

elements.text: for the completed foreclosure, the Servicer may be reimbursed up to 70% of the approved
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure attorney fees with prior written approval from Freddie Mac. To obtain written
elements.label: text

elements.text: approval from Freddie Mac, the Servicer must submit an RPA via the RPA functionality in
elements.label: text

elements.text: PAID.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will not reimburse the Servicer for any fees or costs associated with a restarted
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure if the Servicer has to restart a foreclosure proceeding due to a delay that is not
elements.label: text

elements.text: considered an allowable delay as defined in Section 9301.11(b) or due to Servicer non￾compliance with the Guide.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The States affected by this requirement are identified in Exhibit 57A, Approved Attorney,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Foreclosure, Mediation, Postponement Fees and Title Expenses.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In all other States, Freddie Mac will reimburse the Servicer for only one occurrence of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure attorney fee and reimbursable actual costs (per the requirements of the Guide).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Reimbursement of BPO/appraisal reports
elements.label: text

elements.text: Since the Servicer does not incur an expense when it orders a BPO or appraisal report from
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac via BPOdirect® in connection with a workout or a foreclosure, a BPO or
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraisal report via BPOdirect is not an expense for which the Servicer may seek
elements.label: text

elements.text: reimbursement. Freddie Mac will only reimburse the Servicer for an appraisal report ordered
elements.label: text

elements.text: from an appraiser of its choice if the Servicer has complied with Section 9202.4(a).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) REO related costs and expenses
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will reimburse the Servicer for post-acquisition activities that a Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: perform for Freddie Mac, the MI, the FHA and/or the VA. (Refer to Chapter 9603 for
elements.label: text

elements.text: information on these activities.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9701.4: Non-reimbursable expenses, adjustments and
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: expense offsets (12/17/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Non-reimbursable expenses
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Denials and adjustments of expense reimbursement requests
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Default reporting requirements and reimbursement of claims
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Expense offsets
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Forwarding remittances to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Non-reimbursable expenses
elements.label: text

elements.text: Consistent with Section 8101.1(b), standard operating costs incurred by a Servicer or its
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Agent and/or Outsourced Vendor(s), as applicable, in connection with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer’s obligations and duties owed to Freddie Mac are part of the Servicer’s cost of doing
elements.label: text

elements.text: business and, therefore, are not reimbursable by Freddie Mac unless expressly provided for
elements.label: text

elements.text: otherwise in the Servicing Contract.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Standard operating costs and expenses that are non-reimbursable by Freddie Mac (together
elements.label: text

elements.text: referred to as “non-reimbursable expenses”) include, but are not limited to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Appraisal reports, except when approved by Freddie Mac in advance
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Attorneys fees resulting from correction of matters that should have been resolved
elements.label: text

elements.text: preforeclosure, including any costs to resolve real property title issues that are the result
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Seller’s or Servicer’s actions or inactions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Documentary stamp taxes/transfer taxes/excise taxes on real estate conveyance (Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac is exempt under Title 12 of the United States Code, Section 1452(e)), unless the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer received Freddie Mac’s prior written approval via PAID (Payments Automated
elements.label: text

elements.text: Intelligent and Dynamic) (see Exhibit 88, Servicing Tools). (See Section 9301.10(c) for
elements.label: text

elements.text: additional requirements for reimbursement of transfer taxes after a foreclosure sale or
elements.label: text

elements.text: when closing a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure transaction.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Sales tax
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Facsimile transmission (fax) charges
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Interest, penalties (except for the first tax penalty (and second tax penalty in California as
elements.label: text

elements.text: provided in Section 9701.2(a) incurred as defined in Section 9701.2(a))), late charges,
elements.label: text

elements.text: collection expenses or legal fees for late payment of property taxes or
elements.label: text

elements.text: condominium/homeowners association or Cooperative Corporation assessments unless
elements.label: text

elements.text: otherwise provided in the Guide. (See Chapter 8801 for special Servicing requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: for Cooperative Share Loans.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Long-distance telephone calls
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mailgram charges
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mileage or travel costs
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgage credit life insurance premiums
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Other costs of an attorney, such as time or fees for curing a Delinquency, document
elements.label: text

elements.text: preparation, word processing or notary public services performed by an attorney; co￾counsel fees; referral fees, packaging fees or other similar fees and new case start-up fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Photocopy costs
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Photographs
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Regular postage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The actual or imputed value of in-house counsel time expended when the reimbursable
elements.label: text

elements.text: matter is handled by in-house counsel
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Additional fees for preparing a foreclosure deed because the cost of doing so is included
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the attorney’s fees listed in Exhibit 57A, Approved Attorney, Foreclosure, Mediation,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Postponement Fees and Title Expenses
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Credit reports
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Additional fees or service charges billed by a law firm or any entities the firm relies upon
elements.label: text

elements.text: to provide third-party support functions performed on the Servicer’s behalf that are
elements.label: text

elements.text: considered included in the attorney’s fees listed in Exhibit 57A and Exhibit 57B,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Approved Bankruptcy Attorney Fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Additional fees or service charges that are billed by a law firm or any entity the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: relies upon and are associated with Servicer functions. Servicers should pay vendors for
elements.label: text

elements.text: handling such Servicer functions at their own expense.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For non-reimbursable expenses, Servicers or their Permitted Vendors (see Section 2405.1(b)
elements.label: text

elements.text: regarding use of PAID by Permitted Vendors) should not be requesting Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: written pre-approval by submitting a request for pre-approval (RPA) via the RPA
elements.label: text

elements.text: functionality in PAID for these expense amounts.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac determines that the Servicer has directly or indirectly passed or charged to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac any non-reimbursable expenses or charged Freddie Mac for Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: obligations covered by the Servicing Spread (as set forth in Section 8103.2(b)), then Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac may pursue any or all remedies available under the Guide, other Purchase Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: and applicable law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Denials and adjustments of expense reimbursement requests
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac reserves the right to withhold payment on a reimbursement request if:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Servicer fails to provide Freddie Mac with supporting documentation requested or in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: time frame Freddie Mac requires
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Freddie Mac did not receive the request within the expense reimbursement submission
elements.label: text

elements.text: time frames (see Section 9701.1(e))
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. The request includes expenses that are typically non-reimbursable unless the Servicer has
elements.label: text

elements.text: obtained Freddie Mac’s written pre-approval
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. The request includes expenses for an amount in excess of the expense limits in Exhibit
elements.label: text

elements.text: 57, 1- to 4-Unit Property Approved Expense Amounts; Exhibit 57A, Approved Attorney,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Foreclosure, Mediation, Postponement Fees and Title Expenses; and Exhibit 57B,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Approved Bankruptcy Attorney Fees, unless the Servicer obtained Freddie Mac’s written
elements.label: text

elements.text: pre-approval.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac adjusts or denies the Servicer’s reimbursement request:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Information on the reason why Freddie Mac either adjusted or denied an expense
elements.label: text

elements.text: reimbursement will be available in PAID
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer may resubmit the reimbursement request via PAID provided the
elements.label: text

elements.text: resubmission is within Freddie Mac’s time frame requirements, the Servicer claims
elements.label: text

elements.text: only the disallowed expense and the appropriate documentation is attached and
elements.label: text

elements.text: includes a justification for why the disallowed expense should be reimbursed
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Default reporting requirements and reimbursement of claims
elements.label: text

elements.text: The legal fees and costs the Servicer submits must reflect the same default action dates as
elements.label: text

elements.text: reported via EDR. Failure to report Delinquency, foreclosure, bankruptcy and other relevant
elements.label: text

elements.text: EDR data will result in the adjustment or denial of the request.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Expense offsets
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must deduct certain funds from expenses incurred. These funds include positive
elements.label: text

elements.text: Escrow balances at default, interest on Escrow balances, property insurance and primary
elements.label: text

elements.text: mortgage insurance premium refunds.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer’s requested expense reimbursements must be offset by any positive Escrow
elements.label: text

elements.text: balance. Funds remaining in the Borrower’s Escrow account at default must be accounted for
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the Servicer’s reimbursement request as income to offset expenses. Funds remaining in
elements.label: text

elements.text: a temporary buydown account before the foreclosure action was started must be applied as
elements.label: text

elements.text: set forth in Section 4204.3. Additionally, the Servicer is required to offset any incurred
elements.label: text

elements.text: reimbursable expenses with the proceeds of rental income it receives during the foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: or REO holding period.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage insurance premium refunds should be refunded directly to Freddie Mac by the MI
elements.label: text

elements.text: on the claim for loss. If, however, the MI sends a refund of the mortgage insurance premium
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the Servicer directly, the Servicer must credit the MI premium refund on the claim as
elements.label: text

elements.text: income when it submits a request for expense reimbursement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the following instances, Freddie Mac will bill the Servicer for the funds on the Performing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loans monthly Servicer Billing Statement or the Servicer Non-Performing Loans Invoice,
elements.label: text

elements.text: respectively:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the income reported by the Servicer exceeds the expenses requested
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Servicer has no expenses currently being processed to offset the income that is due
elements.label: text

elements.text: to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Section 9102.1 for information on the payment of the Servicer Billing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Statement and Non-Performing Loans Invoice via an Automated Clearing House draft.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must not offset anticipated or received mortgage insurance claim payments,
elements.label: text

elements.text: property insurance claim proceeds or sales proceeds against any expenses incurred. (Refer to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 9701.4(e) for information on remitting these funds to Freddie Mac.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Forwarding remittances to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer is required to forward, via wire transfer or check, non-REO-related funds to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac (see Directory 5) and REO-related funds to Freddie Mac (see Directory 6)
elements.label: text

elements.text: within 10 Business Days of the Servicer’s receipt of the funds. Such funds typically include,
elements.label: text

elements.text: but are not limited to, mortgage insurance claim proceeds and property insurance claim
elements.label: text

elements.text: proceeds.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must forward the funds to Freddie Mac via check or wire transfer as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For non-REO-related funds, the Servicer must forward the funds via check payable to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation and include the Freddie Mac loan number
elements.label: text

elements.text: along with copies of related documentation with the check
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For REO-related funds, the Servicer must forward the funds via check or wire transfer as
elements.label: text

elements.text: follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Remittances forwarded by check must be payable to the Federal Home Loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage Corporation and include the Freddie Mac loan number along with copies of
elements.label: text

elements.text: related documentation with the check
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Remittances forwarded by wire transfer must reference the Freddie Mac loan number,
elements.label: text

elements.text: address of property and the type of proceeds (e.g., REO repurchases, mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance claim proceeds and property insurance claim proceeds)
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must not offset any expenses against property insurance claim proceeds or
elements.label: text

elements.text: mortgage insurance claim proceeds.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9701.5: Audit and compliance requirements (09/10/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Standard supporting documentation for expense reimbursement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Expense reimbursement audits
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Consent Agreement terms and conditions
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Standard supporting documentation for expense reimbursement
elements.label: text

elements.text: With the exception of Mortgages insured by the FHA or guaranteed by the VA or RHS not
elements.label: text

elements.text: subject to recourse or indemnification, Freddie Mac generally will not require the Servicer to
elements.label: text

elements.text: submit supporting documentation with its expense reimbursement requests and resubmission
elements.label: text

elements.text: of denied reimbursement requests. However, there are situations, such as audits of
elements.label: text

elements.text: reimbursement requests, where Freddie Mac will instruct the Servicer to provide standard
elements.label: text

elements.text: supporting documentation. Standard supporting documentation for expense reimbursement
elements.label: text

elements.text: requests includes, but is not limited to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Proof of all disbursements: Copies of original bills/invoices of services performed for
elements.label: text

elements.text: expenses submitted on the claim that include, but are not limited to, the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Legal fees and costs,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Property inspection,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Property preservation costs,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Primary mortgage insurance premiums, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Condominium/homeowners association (HOA) or Cooperative Corporation
elements.label: text

elements.text: assessments. (See Chapter 8801 for special Servicing requirements for Cooperative
elements.label: text

elements.text: Share Loans.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. A copy of the project declaration highlighting, if applicable, the portion of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: condominium/HOA assessments that may take priority over the Mortgage. For
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Share Loans, refer to Section 8801.2(d) regarding standard supporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation for reimbursement of expenses incurred on a Cooperative Share Loan.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Copy of the Mortgage history beginning 12 months prior to the DDLPI through the date
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac requests the Mortgage history. Proof of payment of normal Escrow items is
elements.label: text

elements.text: not required if shown in the history. However, if property tax payments are not consistent
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the other taxes or if they were paid in a lump sum, the Servicer must submit
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation from the taxing authority providing the breakdown of the tax amount,
elements.label: text

elements.text: including any late charges, penalties and interest (if applicable).
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. The buydown account balance, if applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: For expense reimbursement requests on Mortgages insured by the FHA or guaranteed by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: VA or RHS not subject to recourse or indemnification, Freddie Mac requires the Servicer to
elements.label: text

elements.text: provide supporting documentation, including, but not limited to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A copy of the claim for benefits filed with the applicable entity
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any communication or requests for information from the applicable entity regarding the
elements.label: text

elements.text: claim, including any response, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Documentation showing the itemization of the distribution of the claim payment received
elements.label: text

elements.text: from the applicable entity (e.g., loss claim packet or advice of payment letter)
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the event a Servicer is required to submit supporting documentation for Freddie Mac to
elements.label: text

elements.text: process the claim, the Servicer’s submission must meet the appropriate submission deadline
elements.label: text

elements.text: in Section 9701.1(e) or any deadline given in Freddie Mac’s request for documentation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Expense reimbursement audits
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will perform audits of expenses to ensure compliance with Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements. If Freddie Mac finds that an expense is not in compliance with applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements, Freddie Mac may deny the reimbursement or adjust the claim.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will communicate an audit request to the Servicer via PAID (Payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: Automated Intelligent and Dynamic) (see Exhibit 88, Servicing Tools). The Servicer will
elements.label: text

elements.text: then be required to submit the standard supporting documentation via PAID within seven
elements.label: text

elements.text: Business Days (refer to Section 9701.5(a)). If the Servicer fails to submit the required
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation by the deadline, the Servicer’s expense reimbursement request will be denied,
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the Servicer will not be permitted to resubmit the claim to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac determines through the audit that an expense was not eligible for
elements.label: text

elements.text: reimbursement, Freddie Mac will:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Provide the Servicer with an explanation of Freddie Mac’s audit findings, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Release the expense back to the Servicer or adjust the expense based on Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: audit findings
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac determines through an audit that a previously reimbursed expense was not
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligible for reimbursement, Freddie Mac will bill the Servicer for the amounts due back to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac on its monthly Servicer Non-Performing Loans Invoice. (Refer to Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9102.1 for information on the payment of Servicing Non-Performing Loans Invoices via an
elements.label: text

elements.text: Automated Clearing House draft.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac determines through the audit that Freddie Mac owes funds to the Servicer,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will remit the funds to the Servicer within 30 calendar days of the date Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac notifies the Servicer of the audit findings.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Consent Agreement terms and conditions
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Defined terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: For purposes of this Section 9701.5(c), the following terms have the prescribed meaning:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Definitions from Exhibit 103, Consent Agreement Incorporated Provisions
elements.label: text

elements.text: A
elements.label: text

elements.text: Advances Advances of delinquent principal and interest (subject to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the provisions of Article III of this Exhibit 103),
elements.label: text

elements.text: advances of taxes and insurance, and all other advances,
elements.label: text

elements.text: including foreclosure and liquidation and related
elements.label: text

elements.text: expenses required to be made by Servicer under the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Guide and/or the other Purchase Documents, including,
elements.label: text

elements.text: but not limited to, the requirements of Guide Chapter
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9701, and Guide Sections 8301.4(g) and 7101.6(a), or
elements.label: text

elements.text: other replacement chapters or sections of the Guide
elements.label: text

elements.text: governing reimbursement of advances, with respect to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Covered Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Advance Financing Financing made by Secured Party to Servicer to fund
elements.label: text

elements.text: Advances subject to reimbursement from Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: C
elements.label: text

elements.text: Consent Agreement The agreement entered into by and among Freddie Mac,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer and Secured Party pursuant to which Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac gives its consent to an Advance Financing (as such
elements.label: text

elements.text: term is defined in Exhibit 103) as set forth in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9701.5(c) and Exhibit 103, and any modifications,
elements.label: text

elements.text: supplements or amendments to the same.
elements.label: text

elements.text: R
elements.label: text

elements.text: Reimbursement Amounts Amounts available for reimbursement to Servicer by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac in connection with Advances related to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Covered Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: This Section 9701.5(c) is not a general offering and is only available to specific
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers. This Section 9701.5(c) does not apply unless and until Freddie Mac and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer execute and deliver a Consent Agreement, in form and substance acceptable to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac in its sole and absolute discretion, implementing these terms and conditions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Consent Agreements
elements.label: text

elements.text: With the prior express written consent of Freddie Mac, a Servicer, in order to obtain
elements.label: text

elements.text: Advance Financing, may grant a security interest to a third-party lender in all of such
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer’s present and future rights, as expressly set forth in and subject to the limitations
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Guide and/or the other Purchase Documents, for the reimbursement of Advances
elements.label: text

elements.text: required to be made by the Servicer under the Guide and/or the other Purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documents, including, but not limited to, the reimbursement of expense requirements of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 9701, Servicer advances required in Section 8301.4(g) and transfer of funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements in Section 7101.6(a).
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac consents to an Advance Financing, it will indicate its consent only by
elements.label: text

elements.text: executing a Consent Agreement, which must also be executed by the Servicer and its
elements.label: text

elements.text: Secured Party, in form and substance acceptable to Freddie Mac in its sole and absolute
elements.label: text

elements.text: discretion.
elements.label: text

elements.text: All Consent Agreements must include the following language:
elements.label: text

elements.text: This Consent Agreement incorporates the provisions of Section 9701.5(c) of the Guide
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the provisions of Guide Exhibit 103 by reference and such provisions are a
elements.label: text

elements.text: substantive contractual part of this Consent Agreement such that each of the Servicer and
elements.label: text

elements.text: its Secured Party expressly agrees:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ That Guide Section 9701.5(c) is operative as to the Servicer and its Secured Party and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Each of the Servicer and its Secured Party is bound by the terms and conditions set
elements.label: text
